Bellissima area, per fortuna poco turistica, dove ci si può riposare, osservare il via vai di imbarcazioni, l'aeroporto Marco Polo di Venezia e anche studiare in tranquillità.
Nice art space to get away from the crowds and clear your mind.

Nonostante fossi arrivata agli orari di apertura la galleria era chiusa (ed è successo in due giorni diversi). Ho parlato al telefono con la curatrice della mostra presente in quel momento che è stata molto maleducata. Lo spazio espositivo è veramente molto bello, quello che lascia l'amaro in bocca è la poca empatia e umanità di chi ci lavora dietro.
Una galleria d'arte che attualmente (2022) ospita anche il padiglione Grenada della Biennale

Ho visitato questo luogo di recente in occasione della mostra  " Gesto , forma, astrazione" della scultrice  Marialuisa Tadei ( scultrice) e  del pittore Vito Campanelli, ed è stata una vera sorpresa sia per la validità delle opere esposte che  per la bellezza intrinseca degli spazi espositivi , che permettono una  comoda e piacevole  visione delle opere .

A little bit of orientation needed to be sure which way you go but a very well organized system of Vaporettas to take you around Venice. Not so fast but just enjoy the sightseeing. Possible to sit but sometimes necessary to stand. Get your tickets before getting on board
..ottimo per passeggiate in Venezia
Ein guter Ort um eine Reise zu beginnen.
Ben allestito anche in periodo di pandemia.
Orari sbagliati dei vaporetti a Venezia
Tranquillità e relax
Sempre molto interessante
Got a scarf for 38 euros. The staff was kind and showed everything patiently.

I FELT SO SAD AND MISERABLE IN THIS STORE…🥲I got a scarf that’s made from cotton and wore a few minutes. Then my neck started feeling very itchy!!!! I went back to the store and asked if I could return the scarf. But a lady in store said no. She said it’s because of my  skin problem. HORRIBLE!!!!  I’ve never experienced feeling itchy after wearing a cotton scarf! I’m not happy I had to throw it away. I wanted to get my money back!!
Really lovely silk scarves for reasonable prices. You get what you pay for but I’m excited to wear them!
Beautiful scarf store. A must!

Amazing restaurant tucked away in a far corner of Venice.  Worth the taxi or long walk.  A broad range of flavors blending the chef's Italian background with love of Japanese cuisine.  Evening started with a consume served in beautiful china tea set and finished with a one of kind Panna Cotta.  Must try restaurant.

My wife and I were invited to try the fine dining experience after checking in to our stay at the stunning Venezia Grand Hotel Palazzo dei Dogi.

We opted for the 7 course menu (there is also an 11 course menu), and were not disappointed. The restaurant manager Andrea greeted us straight away and made us feel very welcome. Throughout the evening we were introduced to the sommelier, who paired every dish with a perfectly balanced wine, and the two talented young chefs who created the unique cuisine.

Not only were the food and drink a once in a lifetime experience, the ability of our hosts to communicate to their guests in multiple languages, explaining the concept of each dish and the origin of the ingredients used, demonstrate how passionate they are about what they are doing.

A fantastic experience for any food lover.
We were lucky to assist to this slow opening of Da Lorenzo Fine dining concept by Paulo Airaudo located in NH collection Hotel Palazzo dei Dogi . Great experience . Great value for money .

Nuovo ristorante gourmet a Venezia, all’interno del bellissimo hotel NH Collection Palazzo dei Dogi. Il nome viene dall’incredibile giardino interno, unico a Venezia. L’ambiente è elegante, pochissimi tavoli, molto personale, tutti gentilissimi, preparati, professionali. Il menù degustazione è interessante e i piatti si susseguono mostrando uno chef di carattere, tecnicamente capace, profondo conoscitore delle materie prime e abilissimo negli abbinamenti. Piatti riconoscibili negli ingredienti a nei sapori. E poi scopri che gli chef sono due, giovanissimi, Salvatore e Alfonso, ispirati da un grande chef, Paulo Airaudo, che ha messo la firma sul menù, ma capaci di eseguire in autonomia e alla perfezione tutti i piatti. Mauro, il maitre/ direttore è un intrattenitore piacevolissimo. Si beve anche molto bene con una giovane sommelier che ci sa fare. Conto nemmeno eccessivo per la qualità della serata. In odore di stella Michelin, sarà un punto di riferimento nella Venezia gourmet.

Ho avuto la possibilità di gustare il Fine Dining Tasting Menù del nuovo ristorante Da Lorenzo Al Giardino Segreto nel sontuoso Nh
Collection Palazzo dei Dogi. La proposta gastronomica viene servita in un ambiente molto intimo ed elegante, dal quale si può godere della vista sul romantico Giardino privato di circa 3000 metri quadrati. Il servizio impeccabile unito ad un raffinato mix di sapori e materie prime di eccellente qualità che viene proposto in ogni singolo piatto, rende questa esperienza veramente indimenticabile.

Un’esperienza indimenticabile. In questo angolo nascosto di Venezia si assapora una cucina creativa e rispettosa della materia prima e del territorio, un ponte tra Oriente e Occidente che a ogni morso regala una scoperta. Una cucina dal ritmo perfetto, senza momenti “morti”, capace di entusiasmare davvero. Tutto impeccabile: dai piatti allo staff, fino allo chef, che ti accompagnano dall’inizio alla fine.

In una zona fuori dal caos del turismo, ma assai vicino al Ghetto, arricchito dalla presenza di un antico giardino botanico in ottime condizioni. Personale attento e disponibile, porzioni adeguate e sempre ben presentate in modo assai "comprensibile". Vini perfetti con ampia scelta. Nulla stona in questo intimo ristorante di alta qualità.

Un’esperienza straordinaria, da stella Michelin piena!
Salvo è uno chef di rara raffinatezza e visione, ogni suo piatto sorprende. Il palazzo, poi, lascia davvero senza fiato per la sua eleganza e bellezza. Un luogo e una cucina che consiglio assolutamente.
Read about this palazzo in the Financial Times and went online to book. No website.  Of course the ministry of culture doesn’t actually want to profit from anything as vulgar as revenue.
After a long period it's been restored and now is office location of beni cultural of Venice
One of a beautiful place in Venice
Closed and can’t find the entrance
Ciao!
Nice
Very quat place

Der kleine Garten gehört zur Synagoge des jüdischen Viertels und ist über einen außerhalb des ehemaligen Ghettos zu erreichen. Ein kleines grünes Paradies inmitten der Altstadt. Es gibt eine kleine Ausstellung zur Geschichte des Parks, der nur mit einem Besucherticket zu besichtigen ist. Unbedingt ansehen.

Nos lo encontramos en la parte posterior de la Sinagoga Española. Se visita con la misma entrada.  Es pequeño pero muy coqueto. El jardín tiene el doble propósito de recuperar un área baldía y brindar a la comunidad un lugar con fines educativos y funcional para ceremonias religiosas. Está rodeado de casas altas, pero a penas perceptibles por la abundante vegetación. Cuenta con diversas plantas tanto medicinales como aromáticas. Tuvimos suerte que no entró nadie y pudimos estar disfrutando del silencio, del ambiente y del murmullo de una pequeña fuente del jardín.

J'ai voulu visiter le jardin de cette synagogue avec de bonnes intentions, dans le respect du lieu et de son histoire. Malheureusement, l'accueil que j’ai reçu a été profondément décevant. La personne à l'entrée s’est montrée froide, impolie, et même ouvertement discriminatoire. Je ne m'attendais pas à un tel comportement dans un espace censé être spirituel et accueillant. Cela m'a mis extrêmement mal à l'aise, au point de repartir sans visiter. Je partage mon expérience en espérant qu'elle sera entendue, et que l'accueil puisse mieux refléter les valeurs d’ouverture et de respect que ce lieu devrait incarner.

Petit jardin sympathique très difficile à trouver.
Si tu es dans une toute petite ruelle.
Possède des toilettes
Particolare questo giRdinocon piante officinali ed aromatiche, protetto dalla cornice delle alte case e dalla sinagoga spagnola. Capanna allo interno che ricorda la festa delle capanne ebraica.

The visual quality is good and sound quality is better but the seats are not confirtable at all!!! It was quiet a shocking for me for a i ternational festival which the oldest in the world that kind of services, seats, gap between the seats were provided! There should be more confortable area with more spave between them to watch movies ib a relax mood. It was just like a remote place to watch movies not like an international festival enviroment. I've seen lots of place way above the Sala Guardino, besudes these defects the Venice film festival still rocks and one of the best in the world, I would come back next year for sure with the hope of more confortable salons
Creative contrast place for inspiration
Top

La sala più suggestiva e rilassante del festival! Nell’attesa della proiezione del film puoi rilassarti e meditare grazie ad un’atmosfera di luce naturalistica, a giochi di luce originali e a musica in sottofondo per chi ama la natura e gli animali, soprattutto i volatili e il loro canto delicato e delizioso. Un’esperienza unica nel mondo forse. Il tutto dentro un grande cubo rosso fuoco.

Früh morgens nach Sonnenaufgang erlebt man einen besonderen Moment der Stille. Kein roter Teppich, keine Reporter und keine Stars. Unter den Bäumen stehen die verlassenen UFOS 🛸 - eigentlich ist nichts los hier aber gerade das ist faszinierend 😁🤷‍♂️
Verde è il colore predominante, più piccolina rispetto alle altre sale ma molto bella!

크고 빨간 건물입니다.
가건물의 느낌이고, 상영관 안이 살짝 덥습니다.
베니스 영화제의 주요 상영관 중 하나입니다.

Ho assistito alla proiezione del Leone d'argento. Sala da 500 posti bella e comoda, però secondo me andrebbe rivisto il sistema dell'aria primaria: a sala piena, l'aria diventa un po' malsana e nuoce ai cardiopatici.

Apartamento perfecto para pasar tus vacaciones en Venecia.Tal cuál como se describe en Booking.
Muy limpio y cómodo.Dotado de todo lo necesario para sentirte como en casa. Muy bien comunicado y Siria fue muy amable al facilitarnos la entrada.
Absolutamente recomendable.

Amazing exhibition for the biennale of 2024. The pavilion is very big and host a great selection of works for this yeas show. And I think it’s a excellent curated exhibition. The selection of work is amazing, showing the best contemporary work from around the world. But also some more “classic” 1900’s work.
You need a couple of hours properly experience the whole exhibition. But it is a must in today’s art world.
It like having the creme de la creme under one roof.  Excellent job.

2023's approach of the curator is really nice. And I really praised the central pavilion.
Lesley Lokko did a great job and I would suggest that one checks this one if you happen to be in venice and you have very limited time.
A fact that this contribution could have been more architectural standw r

Really enjoyable experience visiting The Milk of Dreams at this year’s Biennale. Great variation in art from artists from all over the world. The space is located within lovely and spacious gardens, and the building is surrounded by the pavilions of a range of countries - all of which are worth a visit!
Absolutely underrated place to visit.
This was truly a wonderful experience.  So many things to see and read.  Plan on spending at least 4-5 hours if you are fast, but long if you try to read and see everything.  Loved it.

The Biennale is always exciting.
In 2022 to my opinion the "central pavillon" was less interesting than the national pavillon.
UK, FRANCE, DENMARK, USA, KOREA were my favorites
The central pavilion always offers interesting ideas in biennials and brings together common ideas, I really liked the cardboard models

The Main Pavilion is definitely the centrepiece of the Biennale. Whilst the national pavilions are unique in their own, the Main Pavilion is where one discovers a coherent concept which manifests the contemporary institutional perspectives. This year’s exhibition was extremely well curated and original, focusing on women in surrealism. In this respect, it is a reflection of a wider global effort to rediscover the history of surrealism: think of the Tate Modern’s Surrealism Beyond Borders exhibition, for example.
It is large, but manageable. The information is clear, yet succinct - a thing most welcomed by the busy tourist or art lover. Having said this, one needs time. This is definitely not the place to go if you don’t have a rather crystallised idea about the history of art. That is not to say don’t go, but you must have realistic expectations about the minimum amount of time needed. If you’re not that big on contemporary, it may be worth considering visiting smaller, more manageable, and cheaper galleries.
In terms of prices, the Biennale ranks in the upper strata for the budget traveller. Tickets for adults are about €20, whilst students and seniors pay €16. Again, if you’re not that big on contemporary, it may be the best thing to skip this to enjoy Venice more.
The gift shop inside is vulgarly expensive, though, at least for the student budget. Frankly, it’s inexcusable and insulting to the Biennale. Nonetheless, one does not visit to shop, but to look.
Looking may be difficult for the average visitor. Staff are extremely kind and helpful and knowledgeable. Definitely recommend to speak to them and ask questions. The visit instantly gets much better this way, by interacting with the people who essentially run the place.

Panchina A. Nulli: posto da non perdere per avere la completa esperienza di Venezia, per godere il panorama e i tramonti gustando uno spritz. Ideale per un pasto frugale fuori dalla bolgia turistica, però poi portatevi via le immondizie. Attenzione a sedersi, potrebbe arrivarvi sulla camicia un improvviso schizzo di sugo, ma vale l'esperienza. Aggiungi anche tu la tua foto!
Ti siedi in riva al mare, con la brezza marina e la vista di Venezia davanti. Best things in life are free...
Ricordi di panorama vasto e immensamente bello. Le cose migliori della vita sono gratis.
la panchina nulli è uno dei luoghi che preferisco.
Da non perdere assolutamente

We stayed there for a few days and were delighted by our host Lucia who picked us up at the bus station and by the cleanliness and tasteful interior design of the place! The location is great, plenty of excellent restaurants and bars around and very close to bus/train/boat stations. It was really like a nest with view on a canal!
Shower, fridge, AC, everything was perfectly working and the host prepares many small attention that made our arrival and stay even more memorable :)

I don't understand why the reviews are so bad! The service was excellent
excellent. ALSO SUPER NORMAL PRICES THIS CLOSE TO SAN MARCOM. 8 euro for an aperol apritz, 2.5 euro coffee.
The weiterwas very helpful and polite, spoke perfect english. Our sprits were excellent. The toilets were very clean. It is in a very cute park with a hidden cannal
Very good macaron also
Well great location. Reasonable prices considering coffee was excellent and service nice. Enjoyed the red glass tables and the view of the Grand Canal.  Historical setting.  Recommend.
Slow service but good pastries. Due to slow delivery, coffee was only warm and not hot. Coffee is average. Expensive to sit in, take out your food and eat outside by the lake.

We ordered 2 salads, cicchetti and drinks.
Presentation was good, taste was okay. Coffee was decent. It was a bit pricey, but the location is really great. You can look at the water and you’re close to St. Mark’s square.

I had the pleasure of visiting Illy Cafe in Venezia, and I can confidently say it didn’t disappoint. Just by the water, it proved to be the perfect stop before we set off on the ferry to Murano.

From the moment we walked in, the warm and inviting atmosphere welcomed us. The baristas here truly know how to craft a perfect cup of coffee. Service was slow, but the coffee wad outstanding. It completely exceeded my expectations—it was rich, aromatic, and absolutely delightful!

We indulged in some delicious pastries that paired wonderfully with our drinks, making it a lovely mini brunch experience. It was smart of us to stop here; the combination of fantastic coffee and tasty treats set a great tone for the day.

If you find yourself in Venezia near the ferry station, don’t miss out on Illy Cafe. Whether you're a coffee aficionado or just looking for a cozy spot to relax before your next adventure, this cafe is a must-visit. I can't wait to return!

Disaster. Don’t waste your time and money dining here. Coffee isn’t good at all and the service is terrible. The waitress is extremely rude and don’t expect she will assist you for anything. We ordered two drinks and one main to share. She didn’t give you any plate and when you asked, she gave you one plate only lol. No tape water can be provided and there’s only one stilled water in large bottle size which is approximately for 4-6ppl. If you are solo traveller, or just two persons like us, pls don’t ever come here or u will be damn disappointed.

It’s a good place to rest after much walking around Venezia. Food and coffee were average for the price. A cup of Americano is Eur7.  The service is prompt from the waitress to be seated and they come and take your order quite quickly, restaurant has also clean toilets for the diners.

Overpriced! Croissant tasteless, coffee charged for large instead of medium & they style found a way to get out, I was in a hurry so didn't make a point.
Ordered 1 chiquetto, served 2 - 1st & last time for this short venice trip.
Wifi unavailable

Great pizza spot with an extensive menu. One of our party got one of the best cheeseburgers she'd ever had. We started with the fried polenta and it was highly craveable. The pizza was excellent. Great drink selections. Fair prices. Not the kind of place you'd be drawn into from the street (if you could even find it), but if you like casual dining and extremely good pizza, put this place on your list. It's on the eastern end of the island away from the touristy hustle. Many Italians seemed to eat here. The place almost seemed closed from the front, but it had a sizeable dining room that opened up in the back to a fantastic patio with plenty of seating, shady or sunny. Obviously high-quality ingredients and everyone in the kitchen knew what they were doing. Would have eaten here more had we found it sooner.

Beware of their hidden €5 cover charge per person. It’s not mentioned on their menu, website, nor does the wait-staff make this known.

We searched high and low for good pizza in Venice and finally heard about Dai Fioi where we made a reservation. When we arrived, it felt like we walked into the back of the restaurant. We ended up asking the chef if we came in the wrong door? And he pointed us to the bartender.

We were made to wait 5 min for a table despite the fact that there were numerous 2-person tables available which slightly irked us. Then we had to repeatedly wave down our server for help ordering drinks and food and to pay. He was a good server but just inattentive. The pizza options were thoughtful and well put together, but honestly the crust had no integrity and we ended up having to eat the pizza with our fork lol. The flavors were spot on, though.

As we paid the bill, I didn’t recognize “2 x coperto €5” line item. I asked and was told it was their cover charge? This bothered us. Felt like they were taking advantage of the fact that we may have been tourists / non-Italian speakers.

Whatever.

Hosted us without a reservation despite the busiest of afternoons due to the proximity to the marathon finish line.  Great staff and even better food.  The very best meal we had in Venice over the past several days. Good value too.

One of the best pizzas I’ve had in Venice! The place feels authentic, not touristy, and the quality is amazing. The dough was light and perfectly cooked, and everything tasted fresh and delicious. Definitely recommend if you want a real local pizza experience in Venice.

Some of the best pizza I have had! From the get-go Shakil was very attentive to us and very friendly, even getting my mum to order in Italian to everyone's amusement.
Fast service and nice pizzas. Seems like a popular place that can get a bit busy later in the evening.

Service, food, and atmosphere were all top notch. We got a pizza and some pasta. Both dishes were absolutely fantastic. Along with delicious food, the staff were some of the friendliest I've encountered in Venice so far. It's a decent walk from the tourist areas of Venice, but it is well worth it.

Our pizzas were amazing!
We had the Trip to Italy , un classico and the Nerano.
The wait staff were amazing and quick to serve us our pizzas! The Location is tucked away from the crowds so it was nice to slow down and enjoy our dinner!

The pizzas are awesome! Very tasty, the ingredients are super fresh, and the service was extraordinary; our waiter “Shakeel” was incredibly welcoming and nice, we asked for his suggestions and it was top notch.
Absolutely recommended.

We sat down outside for drinks at night. When the waitress came over she was speaking Italian and we asked if she spoke English. She said “oh sorry I told them [the other wait staff] that you were Italian”. We ordered drinks and when they were brought over she asked if we could pay immediately. We were planning on ordering more drinks but thought maybe this what just how this place ran, even though no where else in Italy asks you to pay immediately. After we paid we felt very unwelcome there and ended up leaving. I understand that we are tourists and do not live here, but it is another thing to feel completely unwelcome. We ended up going to dolce vita a short walk away and it was GREAT. Highly recommend dolce vita, but not orange if you are English speaking.

Update 31.12.2024: I came back for breakfast with my girlfriend and we have been quite disappointed. The quality of the food is still so and so but the general mood and service need to be downgraded, the people were exhausted, with no passion nor protocol to follow looking improvised.
.
It is a very popular place in the evening by youngsters qnd students looking for a nice meeting place to drink spritzes and beers at a good price.  My review instead is for the morning breakfast and in particular for the eggs which are rare to find in Venice outside the hotels. The quality of the food is so and so but the view of the field and the people passing by is truly unique.

edit: aggression is so confirmed! stay away from this place - man with tattoos and piercing is dangerously aggressive. Currently sat across from the bar, they have a rooftop above and someone accidentally dropped their water bottle. He came out, picked up the bottle and flung it the complete opposite direction at passerbys walking by. Know when to address your staff and cut the toxicity. What a horrible, horrible excuse of a man. Homophobic, aggressive and completely unfit to be in hospitality.

Original Review (before updating half hour later from across the street(:

tried to buy a glass at the bar - the man instantly said ‘people like you always come to the bar and I have to tell you - go sit outside’, literally shouting at me. When I was confused as to what he said and asked the other server what he was saying he shouted over ‘I told you. you sit outside or go’. By the way, I’m gay and dress a bit feminine - this is quite an older masculine and pretty aggressive guy (if I do say so). Not saying anything, but make up your mind…

When I actually decided to sit down - he said ‘sit’ like a dog. I then thought nah I’m gonna leave, and when I left he said ‘yeah go leave then go on we don’t want you here’ and started shouting at me down the street, getting other tables to laugh. Pretty humiliating lol.

I work in Venice a lot of the year, I’ve been here 7/8 times in the last year alone, and have never been treated so horribly by someone - the other worker apologised and said he was a stresshead. Literally avoid like the plague - to think I was gonna pay for a drink after that, I’m an idiot.

I can’t say anything about the food but the drinks were priced reasonably and the atmosphere at night is great for those looking for a younger crowd. Visited during karaoke night by chance and had a great time. Highly recommended for those looking to meet new people

For a place called Orange, clearly focused on Aperol and likely somehow sponsored by them, in a city like Venice that is super proud of its Spritz, the Spritz I got tasted too watered down.

It also took surprisingly long before our drinks got served.

It's a place that clearly targets students and so it was very popular with them. The result is an incredibly lively atmosphere.

The tables outdoor overlook the entirety of Campo Santa Margherita, one of the largest and busiest squares in Venice.

The place also seems to have a nice terrace on the upper floor.

Horrible service. All the bar tenders are rude. Came in at 1230 and was told they have a wait and they wont be serving anymore while people were still coming in and getting help. To simply put, avoid this place.
Server was rude for no reason to group of students who were very polite and spent a lot of money. Go somewhere else, don’t give them your business. Plenty of other good bars
This place is amazing! Great view of the streets of Venice, rooftop seating area, affordable drinks and food, and excellent service! Also, try the ketchup!

The garden is absolutely gorgeous, with a beautiful purple hanging wisteria that smells divine.
The food was great, although the portion sizes were on the small side for their price.
On the night we visited, the service seemed a bit slow and chaotic, with dishes arriving in random orders, and our mains arriving 1.5 hrs after ordering. I hope this is a one-off incident.
The garden is absolutely beutiful, the fish is fresh, and the "Vino de la casa" was smooth and a perfect pairing with the fish.
9/10 recommended
Charming terrace seating, charming and attentive owner, delicious food. Our tour spent our last evening together having dinner at this lovely restaurant. Thank you!
Food was fine, service was fine, all just fine. The garden side was pretty cool. The service fee was not fine.

We found this restaurant by chance when we were wandering around the neighbourhood. It looked cute and authentic from the front and then the inside garden was beautiful. The food was delicious and well priced. For the two of us it was about €25 each including a bottle of wine to share. The only thing I marked down for was the waiters service, they weren’t too enthusiastic or welcoming but the owner was there and he was hot so maybe I should score it higher.

This place has an amazing garden and we would've probably enjoyed it had it not been for our server. He was really rude from the start and wouldn't even give us menus, even though we were really polite. I don't remember his name, but he made the whole experience really unpleasant. It was like he didn't want customers bothering him.

It wasn't a bad meal. But it came out so late that everyone waited for a long time. Everyone tried to hide their faces, including our table. The cooking time took 30-40 minutes and I lost interest. It's a tourist restaurant, but it takes too long so I don't think I can come twice. Still, the scenery and the employee didn't lose their laughter.

Our favourite meal in Venice. (On the last night- what a way to end our weekend!)
Tasty bread service, and great wine. We had some fantastic tasting pasta dishes, as well as pork cheeks in cabbage and Venetian liver. Grerat host- would definitely recommend (will be a part of our Venice VLOG on our youtube channel when it´s out)

An exceptional yet honest and welcoming family run restaurant. They put their heart into their food and respect traditional recipes with excellent results. We could not believe the quality of the food for the price we paid. The octopus salad was the best we have ever tasted!

Plus the ambiance of the garden are both during the night and in the day is perfect. This place is a hidden gem.
Si ascoltano gli alberi e le foglie. L’atmosfera autunnale è salva. La vista è caratteristica. Pulendo lo scivolo c’è pure modo di far giocare i bombi.
È un luogo che conserva i resti del castello di Mestre .In questo periodo dell' anno è particolarmente suggestivo  .
Bel luogo di tranquillità,  ci sono anche giochi per bambini

Piccolo ma interessante giardino nel cuore di Mestre. Otre ad essere un luogo di relax ci consente di vedere parte delle vecchie mura che circondavano la città e di cui, purtroppo, non è rimasto quasi nulla.
E' stato recentemente riaperto dopo il restauro di parte delle mura e della fontana.

Tranquillo giardino/parco pubblico al centro di Mestre, piacevole la vicinanza dei resti delle antiche mura mestrine. Peccato sia effettivamente infestato da tante zanzare . Potrebbe essere fatto qualcosa per evitare il problema .

Una piccola oasi di verde nel centro di Mestre.
Un luogo adatto per sedersi all'ombra di maestosi alberi e riprendere fiato dalla calura estiva contemplando qualche residuo delle antiche mura della città.
Posto magico di Mestre. È un luogo storico della città. Ci si può sedere nelle panchine sotto alberi secolari

Ieri e oggi, in occasione della festa della Madonna della salute, si è tenuta la manifestazione "il giardino ritrovato" proprio per ridare vita ai giardini e la partecipazione è stata veramente notevole‼️

This was one of the stops on a food tour I took and I was so enamored by the restaurant I decided to come back for my last meal in Venice. On the food tour I enjoyed the spaghetti pomodoro which was so simple yet complex I could have licked the plate clean…or scarpetta as they say in Italian. On my “last meal” visit I enjoyed the porchetta and artichokes as my antipasto and the carbonara for my first. (I’m so full I won’t have a second. I sat outside in the garden and reflected on the beauty that is Venice and the incredible time I’ve had in Italy. This was a fantastic representation of everything I’ve learned about local traditional foods…respectful, classic preparations with incredible skill and nothing fussy or Avante Garde. My food was perfectly cooked, perfectly seasoned and it’s exactly the way I wanted to spend my last day! Grazie mille per la cena perfetta!!!

Very disappointed on all aspect. I’m not sure why the rating of this place is high really… I tried way better places in Venice for same price range. We were 3 people and ordered 3 dishes: supposedly fresh pasta: sauce has literally no taste , almost nonexistent from flavour point of view.
Pizza are similar to New York pizza…
The service is probably the worse part. The waiter aren’t really friendly. We asked for a table outside they said no as all reserved… the entire time we had lunch  the tables were empty… very disappointed

Fried Seafood Plate was light and flavorful as was the roasted sea bream. Grilled veggies and garden salad sides were a welcome addition after a heavy carb and meat / seafood menu for the last few days.  The service we had was fantastico thanks to the time our excellent waiter took with us.  His family owned the building and we basically asked him to plan our menu. It was delicious.

Lovely ambiance for sure! The pasta with seafood was so tasty! The squid and the branzino were not as good, I wasn't too much of a fan. Decent service. Was recommended to go to this place by a local! Its set in a garden environment which is rare for Venice.

I had such a wonderful experience at Nono Risoto in San Marco! The customer service was excellent—so welcoming and attentive. I ordered pasta, and it was absolutely delicious, cooked to perfection with amazing flavors. Definitely a place worth visiting if you’re in Venice! 🍝🇮🇹

Food was awesome! Good service, the staff was friendly and quick. The food came surprisingly fast although a lot of other guests. The atmosphere was incredibly amazing and cozy! The Italian garden style made it even more charming!

Beautiful outdoor garden area. Food was good. We had the mixed fry & mussels for appetizers! House sparkling white wine was ✨
Service was ok. You can visibly see they treat locals better. We saw that in Rome and Venice
Great food and service, recommend a reservation if you want to sit outside. Personally found the 2016 american pop music playlist was a weird choice though and the toilets need a bit more care.

It was a fantastic meal, I have spent 4 weeks in italy, and this is a great one to start my 1st day in Venice. Staff members are friendly and welcoming, even make great suggestions. The creamy pistachio scallop pasta was top notch, very creamy, light yet fragrant, the black pasta with spider crab was also bursting with flavours, rich and bright with the red sauce.

This is the best restaurant we’ve eaten in Venice or possibly our whole entire Europe trip thus far!! Our server recommended a pistachio scallop pasta dish that was absolutely divine!!! I do not give 5 stars easily and this is the only place I’ve given it to!! My partner absolutely loves the vongole pasta. We had it before going into Venice island and we just had to have it when leaving as well. The handmade cake was absolutely lovely as well. Very lemony and moist. If you have to go to 1 restaurant in Venice, please come here!! You won’t regret it!! We will definitely be back!!

I had a starter grilled calamari and squid nice also bruschetta . Mains I had spaghetti with shrimp and half lobster white sauce. Also risotto zucchini and shrimp and desserts very tasty pistachio cheese cake and lemon cake. Also bottle of wine all that for €131 very tasty food and nice wine.
Good service short wait for food lovely place.

Our first night we came across this enchanting restaurant with a garden, private outdoor area once inside the restaurant.

Unlike others, their outside seating was a wonderful canopied garden.  My children had lots of fun.

Food was excellent and the sea bass with vegetables given as a suggestion by the staff was fantastic.

Highly recommend the place as my family was very happy with our dinner experience.

Thanks

I recently had the pleasure of dining at Spice Garden Restaurant, and it was a truly delightful experience. The ambiance was cozy and welcoming, with soft lighting and tasteful decor that created a relaxing atmosphere.

The staff was friendly and attentive from the moment we arrived. Our food was served quickly and was absolutely delicious. I ordered the grilled chicken with mashed potatoes and a side of sautéed vegetables — everything was cooked to perfection.

The flavors were rich but not overwhelming, and the presentation of the dishes was elegant. The price was also quite reasonable considering the quality of both the food and service.

Overall, I would definitely recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a comfortable place with great food and excellent service. I’ll surely be coming back again!

restaurant ribo was a highlight of our visit. The food was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully prepared. The service was warm and welcoming, and the atmosphere felt cozy and authentic. We’d go back in a heartbeat!

The terasse at the back of the restaurant is so nice. The greenery and the lights give a nice ambiance. My mom ate a bowl of mussels and we shared a platter of Tuna (chef recommended) and it was soooo delicious!! I totally recommend and concerning the price, its cheaper than a lot of restaurants in Venice!

The environment of the restaurant was giving a green glow,feel so fresh. Separately, we have to talk about Mixd cold cuts platter with cheese. The taste was mind blowing.
Staff was so friendly.

Amazing pizza and the dough was lovely and thin and light so didn’t feel too heavy! First day of our holiday food a success! Had the specie burrata pizza and added sausage - so tasty! Great atmosphere and whilst we were too full it appears the tiramisu is popular in here!

A great find in the back streets of Venice. One of the tastiest pizzas I’ve had. They are great value and really big! It looks like a family run restaurant, the staff were very welcoming, spoke perfect English and were very helpful. Two pizzas, a salad, two glasses of wine, water and two coffees came to €55.
I liked the food there, pizza with seafood was great, salad also. Service and price is good, the portions are large, you can easily share. I recommend❤️

We had a great experience at this trattoria. We ordered the Pizza Diavola along with two others (whose names we can’t remember but were equally delicious), two local beers recommended by our friendly waitress, and ended the meal with a classic tiramisu. The food was tasty, the service was quick and attentive, and the atmosphere felt welcoming. Prices were average for the region and well worth it for the quality and experience. Highly recommend if you’re in Venice looking for a solid, satisfying meal.

This place is a hidden gem.

No bouncers outside asking tourists to come in. Instead, just a quiet place, great air conditioning and amazing pizzas.

Both adults and kids loved the pizzas and to be honest they were pretty standard Italian pizzas but they used quality ingredients, good mozzarella and they were baked perfectly. It was how eating a pizza in Italy should be.

The service was friendly and efficient and I would love to go back here for dinner.

Delicious spot for pizza! Cute little spot, the patio was closed because it was too hot. Otherwise that would’ve been a cute area to sit as well!
Must try the Tedesca! It will allow you, the flavor is great! The Bufaolina was also really good !
Wow!  This place is great.  The pizza is very good.  Staff are friendly.  Well worth the few steps off the main path.

The BEST pizza I’ve ever had. So delicious. The base wasn’t floppy and had a nice crunch. The sauce had a lot of flavour and it didn’t even need any condiments.

Staff were lovely. Gave us recommendations for exploring Venice. Would definitely go again. Miss the pizza already! Hard to find it like that in the UK.

Also SO cheap!!!

It was a fantastic meal, I have spent 4 weeks in italy, and this is a great one to start my 1st day in Venice. Staff members are friendly and welcoming, even make great suggestions. The creamy pistachio scallop pasta was top notch, very creamy, light yet fragrant, the black pasta with spider crab was also bursting with flavours, rich and bright with the red sauce.

This is the best restaurant we’ve eaten in Venice or possibly our whole entire Europe trip thus far!! Our server recommended a pistachio scallop pasta dish that was absolutely divine!!! I do not give 5 stars easily and this is the only place I’ve given it to!! My partner absolutely loves the vongole pasta. We had it before going into Venice island and we just had to have it when leaving as well. The handmade cake was absolutely lovely as well. Very lemony and moist. If you have to go to 1 restaurant in Venice, please come here!! You won’t regret it!! We will definitely be back!!

Good service short wait for food lovely place.

Unlike others, their outside seating was a wonderful canopied garden.  My children had lots of fun.

Food was excellent and the sea bass with vegetables given as a suggestion by the staff was fantastic.

Highly recommend the place as my family was very happy with our dinner experience.

Thanks

The staff was friendly and attentive from the moment we arrived. Our food was served quickly and was absolutely delicious. I ordered the grilled chicken with mashed potatoes and a side of sautéed vegetables — everything was cooked to perfection.

The flavors were rich but not overwhelming, and the presentation of the dishes was elegant. The price was also quite reasonable considering the quality of both the food and service.

Overall, I would definitely recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a comfortable place with great food and excellent service. I’ll surely be coming back again!

restaurant ribo was a highlight of our visit. The food was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully prepared. The service was warm and welcoming, and the atmosphere felt cozy and authentic. We’d go back in a heartbeat!

The terasse at the back of the restaurant is so nice. The greenery and the lights give a nice ambiance. My mom ate a bowl of mussels and we shared a platter of Tuna (chef recommended) and it was soooo delicious!! I totally recommend and concerning the price, its cheaper than a lot of restaurants in Venice!

The environment of the restaurant was giving a green glow,feel so fresh. Separately, we have to talk about Mixd cold cuts platter with cheese. The taste was mind blowing.
Staff was so friendly.

Un frutteto e un giardino bellissimi, profumati e tranquilli, con panchine dove rilassarsi dal trambusto di Venezia. Ospita anche un bar e una sala espositiva, una cappella e altri elementi conventuali. Una terrazza con pergolati e panchine si affaccia sul Lido e sulle altre isole della Laguna di Venezia. Una Madonna veglia sul tramonto sulla riva meridionale della Giudecca. Un'esperienza sensoriale e terapeutica per i viaggiatori. Un privilegio assoluto.

Dopo la visita alla basilica,si scende le scale,si prende la destra e si entra in un orto giardino ricchissimo di flora. Ad accogliervi ci saranno anche 2 volontari ed un bar.poi,diverse panchine x una lettura riparati dall' uva o fronte laguna

Der Garten (halb Nutz- und halb Ziergarten) des zur Redentore-Kirche gehörigen Klosters. Eingang links neben der Kirche (Sackgasse "Calle dei Frati"). Eintritt ziemlich teuer (> € 10,-), der Garten ist aber sehr schön und für Venedig etwas Seltenes. Insbesondere die Uferpromenande (unter einer Pergola) ist sehr lauschig - Blick südwärts auf die Lagune. Kleines Café.

Un luogo magnifico, dove si respira tranquillità pace e si sta bene. Ci sono stato per alcuni lavori che ho avuto il piacere di svolgere. il lavoro dei Fratelli è infinito: dentro e fuori il Convento. Persone speciali!

Un posto incantato tra pace silenzio e religione. I frati cappuccini sono molto gentili a far da Cicerone nella visita. Molto interessante il refettorio con alle pareti appesi i dipinti dei vecchi priori del convento. Un bellissimo giardino con i "semplici", ulivi e vigneto. Dalla balaustra a sud un meraviglioso sguardo verso le isole della laguna sud. C'è una Cavana con una bellissima Caorlina...il frate mi ha detto che ha partecipato alla vogalonga di Venezia.
Un luogo incantevole, di una bellezza che consola

Situato in un luogo di favola dentro la favola (Giudecca, Venezia), questo luogo di culto dà modo di riconnerci col Divino in mezzo al trambusto di un'isola così turistica. Pur di grandi dimensioni, permette il raccoglimento, con un occhio anche all'arte. Vi sono infatti opere della scuola di Paolo Veronese di Jacopo Palma il Giovane e Francesco Bassano. Alla chiesa del Redentore è annesso il convento dei frati Cappuccini, con lo studentato teologico. Per chi ha la fortuna di accedervi, in fondo al giardino si gode una vista sulle isole della Sacca Sessola, delle Grazia e di San Clemente. È pure annessa una cavana, in cui i frati tengono una Carolina con la quale partecipano alle regate in Canal Grande.
Ein großartiger Garten! Und eine fantastische Aussicht auf die Lagune.

The Countess and her cousin gave us a lovely and intimate tour of her home.  She showed us her priceless heirlooms -- among them Venetian glass chandeliers, 16th Century tapestries, and an aristocrat's gondola sledge (chair).   Her splendid garden is the largest in Venice. The balconies offer views of the Grand Canal.   She served cake, cookies, tea, and cappuccino.  Palazzo Nani Bernado would be a lovely, romantic place to stay while in Venus.
Best private garden in Venice -stunning.
Friendly staff spoke english and the food was excellent!!

Splendido palazzo del XVI secolo, affacciato sul Canal Grande.
Le sue sale con antichi decori e stucchi pregiati, possono essere affittate per matrimoni ed eventi privati.
Dispone di uno dei piu' grandi giardini privati di Venezia.
Bellissimo!! Palazzo privato con un giardino segreto sorprendente, uno dei più belli di Venezia. Merita una visita
Palazzo meraviglioso. Ci siamo stati per un evento privato.

Un pezzo di storia di Venezia. Palazzo storico che si affaccia sul Canal Grande: e con questo il resto è solo secondario. Ma voglio evidenziare che anche questo palazzo, ed è un segreto di questa meravigliosa città,  nasconde aldilà delle sue invalicabili mura un tesoro nascosto...Un giardino meraviglioso ed impensabile, un vero gioiello di spazio e di natura che meraviglia qualunque viandante, veneziano o turista che sia...

Waren 1 Monat in der Wohnung.Januar 2025.Sehr vieles funktionierte nicht,wie Heizung,Geschirrspühler,Wlan Kochherd nur zwei Gasflammen ,Licht in der Küche über dem Gasherd,Sauberkeit....teilweise in sehr desolaten zustand wie Badezimmerfenster Vorhänge und Hauseingang völlig von Taubendreck verschmutzt.Kein Staubsauger den man benutzen konnte..etc.Positiv ist die wundervolle Lage direkt am Canale Grande.Auch der Garten war schön wenn er dann offen war.

Piccolo ma delizioso giardino pubblico inserito a margine del trafficatissimo piazzale Cialdini e dotato di comodi posti a sedere, a forma di bianchi massi. L'area è popolata da splendidi alberi di magnolia le cui caratteristiche e fioriture sono illustrate in un cartellone didattico in loco, nel quale è riportata una mappa del luogo e schede tecniche ben scritte.

I had a delightful time staying here. Rooms were clean and in my opinion it’s well located, the water bus is a six minute walk away and while it is a 30min walk to the main tourist attractions, part of the whole attractiveness of Venice is its winding paths and bridges, which you get to see on the way. There are still very nice restaurants, cafes, and bars 5-6 minutes walking. You get to stay in a much more relaxed area of Venice, with a gorgeous garden area that I had a picnic in twice. I stayed here solo, everyone was very nice, and the view from my room was incredible.

We stayed at the Casa Cardinal Piazza in August 2023 and we had a really nice stay, thank you to the kind and attentive Sisters who could not have been nicer!||Easy to reach by taking the Alilaguna A-line from Marco Polo airport to Madonna dell' Orto pier, the Cardinal Piazza is just around the corner.||The garden of the house is a fabulous oasis in the summer heat and Cannaregio is a quiet sestiere, ideal for relaxation after visiting museums, with good restaurants close by ( good fish dishes from the Diana )||The house is a bit run down and in dire need of new plumbing and renovation, but clean, comfortable and in a good location.||The sisters who run the house do not speak English so brush up your Italian skills. ||They are, however, really nice and kind and ever so helpful.||And remember, curfew at 23:00, so don`t go partying or staying out all night;p||Breakfast, croissants, cake and cereal. Lunch and dinner were good Italian home-cooked meals.||Four stars for cleanliness, kind and good service and calm athmosphere.|

Clean and pleasant convent with a garden (very rare for Venice). Reasonably priced.

However, taking a star off for how much they messed up my booking. When I paid the balance of the reservation upon my arrival at the convent, the extremely unpleasant receptionist somehow put it through onto the wrong account, so that the convent could not receive the money.

They then asked me to pay **again** the same amount - even though the money had already been taken out of my account once already!

Luckily I was staying there for a week, and so refused, and said that if the money was refunded to my account (which it subsequently was) I would put through the transaction again (which I then did). But a lot of aggro that went on for the entire week that I stayed there…

We stayed for one night at this monastery||||Location : The monastery is located close to Chiesa Madonna Dell'Orta, in a very quiet neighbourhood. The Alilaguna(orange from airport, first stop) & ACTV stop is a 4 min walk from the monastery.||||Rooms : We were offered a room that is used by the clergy , since the usual rooms were under renovation. We had nothing to complain because the room was very big, something we would have never managed at any of the other hotels or B&B. The room was clean.||||Amenities : The breakfast spread was sufficient and consisted of the usual stuff, cereals , bread , butter , jam , cheese, Panettone( could be because of Christmas) and fruits. A special mention needs to be made of the garden which is great for walks.||||The place is run by nuns who make sure that you have a comfortable time at the monastery. I would definitely recommend this place to anyone looking for a stay on the islands if one is fine to stay in a monastery.

This is a quite lovely place to stay. The sisters were welcoming and hospitable, the room was simple but comfortable, and the grounds were beautiful and well kept. It's in a quiet part of the city which we also appreciated. Highly recommended.

A rather bad experience. 30 min walking distance from the center. If you go to stay there at eleven o'clock at night they close, you can't leave later than that or enter after.
It is shameful when you are paying for an accommodation in 2024 that they have a curfew, specially when is kept funded by dedicating to this.

While the monastery is very easy to find in Venice and the welcome was lovely that's where it ended.  My room had a musty odour and on the first night my room was cold so I spoke with one of the nuns who explained the heaters are on a timer.  The next night was really cold in my room, long story short the nuns waved off my concerns about being cold and my room being cold but the deal breaker was the tepid shower which the nuns tried to pass off as normal and acceptable.||I left after two nights to stay in a warm and comfortable hotel around the corner for not a lot more money.||There is a lot of work to be done at the monastery to bring the rooms up to scratch and I honestly would have thought the nuns would be more caring about my discomfort rather than shrugging shoulders and telling me it is cold outside so it is cold inside - the organisation asked me to speak with the nuns who would provide me with blankets.  Blankets aren't going to keep me warm or comfortable while sitting in the room, or make the shower have hot water.||I am really disappointed in Casa Cardinal Piazza because my experience staying in monasteries in Italy have been positive in the past, I would never stay at this monastery or suggest anyone stay at the monastery every again.

Yesterday we arrived in Venice and we normally stay at the madonna del orta in Venice a beautiful monastery |But on the internet we had seen this property and looked at the photos of the beautiful garden and the photos of the accomodation which at the time presented well on the internet|Yes there was a lovely welcome from the nuns , but once they had taken us to our room which was tiny for 85 euros |There was a distinct dirty musty unpleasant odour in the bathroom and we just couldn't stay here|My recommendation is that the nuns though very hospitable really need to pick up these game and make some drastic changes to bring these rooms into the 20 th century |They have a great opportunity here but there is lots of hard work to do before this |place is up to a standard where customers can be comfortably accommodated |As regular tourists travelling in Italy Rome Venice florence sicily etc we have spent most of our time staying in convents and monastery's |This convent  might be okay for back packers |You shouldn't be asking customer s to pay this price for such a low standard of accomodation. |I write this review to support all the nuns priests and staff who do a wonderful job keeping their premises to a quality and compliant standard this convent needs to follow them
Qualsiasi zona verde a Mestre è benvenuta. Questa però non è valorizzata a sufficienza.
Area verde in piazzale San Lorenzo Giustiniani
Discreto.poco frequentato.
Niente di speciale
Posto e proprietarie top !!!!!

I appreciate the open space next to the playground. However, I wish the city would allocate a bit more funding to maintain it better. Venice seems to have ample resources, yet it's disheartening that they don't invest more in greenery and parks, which contribute to the city's overall prosperity.
Tried to book to visit but no one could be bothered to respond to my request to visit . Very disappointed
Relax

Il Brolo degli Scalzi è un gioiello che si trova a pochi passi dalla stazione di Santa Lucia ed è tutt’oggi coltivato dai Frati Carmelitani. Terzo elemento di un trittico composto dalla Chiesa e dal Convento è circondato da alte mura che nei secoli l’hanno protetto dagli sguardi indiscreti. Nel giardino si trova un vigneto con più di 20 varietà recuperate da orti e giardini veneziani per ritrovare le antiche viti della Serenissima. Consiglio vivamente di farsi accompagnare da una guida che vi farà anche scoprire la magnificenza della Chiesa degli Scalzi.

Ho prenotato una visita alla chiesa di Santa Maria di Nazareth e al giardino mistico. La guida è stata molto esaustiva è gentile: è stato un piacere passare due ore circa in sua compagnia. Consiglio la prenotazione sicuramente perché è l'unico modo per poter accedere al giardino inoltre è molto interessante per poter approfondire la visita della chiesa, ma anche per chi è interessato alla botanica in generale.

Un momento di pace!
Quando i rumori si fanno opprimenti, riuscire a ritrovare la tranquillità è essenziale!
Questo giardino è un momento di pace che permette di rigenerarsi e riprendere fiato. si nota le cure amorevoli e la dedizione di chi ogni giorno si dedica non solo alla cura del giardino, ma anche all'accoglienza delle persone che spinte dalla curiosità, varcano le soglie di questa piccola oasi. E' un percorso spirituale ma anche solo umano . Da vedere assolutamente.
Comodissimi gli orari, che ogni 40 minuti circa, introducono il visitatore e il curioso ad immergersi in un breve cammino nel verde e nel silenzio.

Da tanto volevo visitarlo. Uscendo dalla stazione ho visto aperto e ho scoperto che in quella giornata facevano visite guidate gratuite. Visita esaustiva con spiegazione della struttura del giardino e delle piante profumatissime. Il giardino non è solo da guardare ma anche da odorare!  Qui viene coltivata la melissa con la quale si fa la famosa acqua di melissa  che ancora oggi si può trovare nella stanza attigua alla  stupenda chiesa degli scalzi.Ottimo anche il rinfresco offerto ai visitatori. Esperienza non pianificata e per questo ancora più apprezzata

Segundo o site "O JARDIM MÍSTICO
REABE PARA VISITAS NA PRIMAVERA", e é necessário agendar previamente. O endereço indicado no mapa é privado, provavelmente há outra forma de ingressar quando é agendada a visita.

This is an ancient building with all the comforts of a 4-5 stars. Service is decent and breakfast satisfying. The best rooms are on the second floor so book in advance. The location is great, just 15 minutes walk to Rialto bridge and 20 minutes to San Marco square.  The garden is wonderful and a true piece of history. If Venice is your destination, try this hotel.

Wonderful stay at the Palazzo Abadessa
I stayed at the Palazzo Abadessa Hotel with my children, and we had a fantastic time. My children were especially happy – the entire staff was incredibly friendly, helpful, and polite. They made us feel truly welcome.
The hotel's location is also perfect: very central and ideal for exploring the city.
We would return anytime and highly recommend the hotel!

I know this is very subjective and many would not agree, but this is how I imagine a quality hotel. It had friendliest staff as we were there and the historical palace is just magical. You can touch history here... Wait until you discover the garden!
Yes, the green and very quiet garden was the suprise of the year that I did not expect in Venice.
The hotel is away from the busy street yet you can reach Rialto quickly. Breakfast was great. Loved the interior!

Charming old hotel with lots of “typical Italian” decorations. Great if you want to really go for that look aesthetically! The manager was literally so wonderful that it made me leave this review. Would recommend based on his personality alone.

Be careful with this place. They will offer you “free upgrade” at check in then try to charge you next day. Saw it happen to two others arguing at check in then tried to sneak it on us by saying it would be easier to carry bags there (they didn’t help us carry bags up the stairs in an attempt to sell)

Rooms are tiny and AC / Wifi doesn’t work. Breakfast is gross.

Staff tried to collect city tax three times even though I showed I paid.

Better options all around for the price.

I had the most enchanting and magical stay at Hotel Palazzo Abadessa. ||When I arrived at the palazzo, I was wondering if I really deserved this. It was a beautiful and real palazzo dating back to 16 th century.   I chose this hotel, since I wanted to experience the real culture and history of Venetian Palazzo. It turned out be more than I had expected.||It felt like staying at your Venetian dream home away from your average home. One of staff said to me when I arrived. "It is your home, "||It has a beautiful garden which is very rare in Venice. And the first floor has lots of Master peace renaissance paintings of Tintoretto and old-world furniture.  I was very lucky to have a very spacious room with old glamour and charm of Venetian decor. It was very specious with a canal view. I am sure I was upgrade due to my gold member status of reward program. However, they did not even mention it. ||The location is perfect with easy walking distance to all the major attractions such as Doger's palace, St Marco Basilica /piazza and Rialto Bridge.   However, it offers a quiet sanctuary from maddening tourist crowd.||What makes this Palazzo very special is the warm hospitality and kindness of the host and staff. In fact, this Palazzo had a real human soul and kind spirit.||The host Jasmine, she was always available to help guests and make your stay better more pleasant, she even told me, if you get lost, call us, we will find you and get you home.  "We will go there together tomorrow to get it all sorted" when I was looking for mobile phone sim provider.  Jasmine has such a genuine humility and kindness. ||I cannot thank staff especially Navid enough, he was my first point of contact. He was the one of the most caring men I 'ever met. Before my visit to Venice, I had a very awful experience of having my phone stolen in Barcelona.  He genuinely wanted to help me to recover from my distress. He was compassionate, kind and helpful trying to make things better for me the whole time. Other staff Lucas and Juan are also nice and kind.||I want to return my Venetian dream home Palazzo. I miss the heart and soul of the real Palazzo Abbadessa.

This hotel is fabulous. We spent 4 days here and loved every minute. I cannot praise the staff and owners enough. All the staff were very friendly, knowledgable and willing to help. I was poorly during our stay ans spent 2 days confined to the hotel. The staff brought me lemon and honey drinks and were very kind. Thank God for the garden and good weather.|Our room on the ground floor was lovely and we both felt very comfortable, Simone, the owner, said she wanted us to feel this gorgeous 500 year old palace was our home!  I wish,

This was our first trip to Venice made even more wonderful by our stay at Palazzo Abadessa.  I could cite the superb location, a haven of peace and tranquility, yet only 100m from the main thoroughfare and Grand Canal; the elegant tasteful interior, lobby and rooms; the serene peaceful garden where we enjoyed breakfast and drinks throughout the day and evening; the generous and tasty breakfasts; but more important than all this, all true nevertheless, was the unwavering attentiveness and politeness of the staff. I simply cannot fault their wonderful welcome and care. Thank you to all.
Stuart & Sue

place_title: Arte nel giardino di Davide Polesel
text: top ❤️

place_title: Arte nel giardino di Davide Polesel
text:
Ottimo lavoro!

Davide e la sua collaboratrice hanno effettuato un lavoro di pulizia di un'area inutilizzata ed incolta.
Hanno fissato due date per gli interventi.
Causa maltempo hanno riprogrammato il lavoro svolgendolo addirittura in anticipo rispetto ai tempi previsti, rispettando pienamente gli orari e gli impegni presi!
Il lavoro è stato svolto a regola d'arte.

text:
Booked an apartment to visit venice. The 5 minute walk to the next ferry and 30 minutes on the direct ferry was very comfortable.

But the service of the place itself was terrible. Over the few days we stayed, they added random new costs without prior notice at the booking. The facilities were very basic and you need to bring the necessities like toilet paper, soap and bedsheets if you dont want to be charged extra.

Additionally, the mattresses were extremely uncomfortable.

Overall, it fulfilled its purpose of doing some day trips to venice but if you want a comfortable stay, we would recommend to search for another place.

A 5min walk will bring you to the Jettys to  catch the ferry to the islands.

We highly recommend Marisa accommodation and will definitely return on our next visit to the area.

Die Zimmer sind schon etwas in die Jahre gekommen, aber die Zimmer sind mit dem notwendigsten ausgestattet und sehr sauber! Parkplatz direkt vorm Haus!
5min zur Schiffsanlegestelle

Die Unterkunft war in Ordnung.  Das Bett war steinhart und es war eiskalt drinnen.  Die Heizkörper waren zwar warm, jedoch reichte die Heizleistung nicht aus um die Unterkunft warm zu machen. Wir verstehen dass es draußen kalt war, aber wenn man etwas im Februar vermietet, sollte man auch dafür sorgen, dass die Wohnung warm ist.
Die Bettwäsche und Handtücher sind nicht im Preis (8 Euro/ Tag).
Die Vermieterin war sehr nett, hat gut deutsch gesprochen und die Außenanlage war sehr schön und gepflegt.

Wir haben spontan angefragt und sehr rasch Antwort bekommen. Haben das Dachzimmer gebucht, ohne Küche, aber mit Bad.  Zimmer und Bad sehr sauber. Alle sehr Freundlich. Fähre gleich um die Ecke.  Alles für einen spontan Kurzurlaub top.

Ubytování bylo na příjemném místě, na pláž se dá na půjčeném kole dostat do 6 minut, k trajektu je to ještě blíž. Vybavení je jednoduché, ale v pořádku. S majitelkou se dalo bez problému domluvit, angličtina i němčina není problém.

Za co nicméně strhávám hvězdy je za skryté poplatky, teprve den před ubytováním nám majitelka napsala, že si nad rámec zaplacené ceny bude účtovat poplatek za ložní prádlo a na místě jsme potom ještě zjistili, že klimatizace nemá ovladač, ten nám dala až po vyžádání a opět za poplatek za každou noc.

Vůbec by mi nevadilo rovnou zaplatit víc, ale tenhle přístup mi přišel odporný, skoro jsem čekal, jestli nám na konci pobytu naúčtuje stočenou vodu a opotřebení brzd na kolech... Nicméně kromě toho bylo ubytování pěkné a můžu ho doporučit.

Campo San Bortolomio is a charming and bustling square in Venice, perfectly situated near the iconic Rialto Bridge. Surrounded by elegant boutiques and high-end shops, it is a lively meeting point where both locals and visitors gather to soak in the city’s vibrant atmosphere. The square’s energy is enhanced by its rich history and architectural beauty, making it an essential stop for anyone exploring Venice.

Fittingly, amidst the luxury and grandeur, you will find a statue dedicated to none other than Giacomo Casanova, the infamous Venetian adventurer and seducer. His presence in this sophisticated setting feels almost poetic—after all, Casanova was known for his love of fine things, from fashion to literature. The statue serves as a reminder of Venice’s alluring past, where romance, intrigue, and elegance intertwined seamlessly.

Whether you’re pausing to admire the statue, indulging in some high-end shopping, or simply enjoying the lively atmosphere, Campo San Bortolomio offers a perfect blend of history and modern charm. Its location near the Rialto ensures it remains a popular spot, yet it still retains a unique character that makes it worth more than just a passing glance.

During the walk from St. Mark's Square to the Academy Bridge, we pass this beautiful square. This elongated square is one of the most elegant in Venice. Campo Santo Stefano or Campo Morosini is one of the largest fields in Venice. It is surrounded by beautiful churches and palaces such as the church of Santo Stefano, the church of San Vidal, Palazzo Morosini, Palazzo Loredan and Palazzo Pisani and the adjacent Campiello Pisani. It is a favorite gathering place of locals and guests, sometimes concerts, Christmas and other fairs are held on this square. And it is certainly very interesting to walk here during the Carnival. In the center of the field stands the statue of the writer Niccolò Tommaseo, the work of Francesco Barzaghi from 1882. The pleasant atmosphere is complemented by coffee bars, patisseries and restaurants such as "Ristorante Pizzeria Santo Stefano", Ristorante "Le Café", Ristorante " A Beccafico ", Ristorante / Bar" Art Blue Café ", Osteria" Ethnos ", Osteria" Doge Morosini ", Gelateria" Paolin "and on the corner and opposite the church the popular Bar" all'Angolo ". Right behind the “Art Blue Cafe” and Palazzo Morosini Gatterburg is the far-famed Venetian Music Conservatory of Music Benedetto Marcello. Every time we pass there we hear good music spreading towards the square from the building. It is interesting and good to know that the Church of San Vidal is a place where classical music concerts of the famous Rondo Veneziano are held. The pride of the church is its very fine Gothic portal, created in 1442 by Bartolomeo Bon. The interior is decorated with ingenious works by Tintoretto.

Il Campo de la Maddalena è una piazza situata nel sestiere di Cannaregio a Venezia. È caratterizzata dalla presenza della Chiesa della Maddalena, che le dà il nome. La chiesa, dedicata a Maria Maddalena, risale al XII secolo, ma è stata ricostruita nel XVIII secolo in stile barocco. Il campo è un luogo incantevole, con il suo caratteristico campanile e la presenza di alcuni caffè e ristoranti che lo rendono un luogo piacevole per rilassarsi e ammirare l'atmosfera veneziana.

Campo antistante la famosa chiesa massonica dedicata alla Maddalena.

Ammirevole il pozzo (tipico di quasi tutti i campi veneziani) per la raccolta acque, con scavata la "ciotola" per l'acqua dei gatti.

After Piazza San Marco, the Campo Santi Giovanni e Paolo was considered by the Venetians, for various reasons, the most important and famous field in the city. First of all, because in this place solemn feasts and ceremonies were often organized and then because the bodies of the Doges and high representatives of the Republic passed through this field before receiving the last anointing in the adjacent Basilica of Santi Giovanni e Paolo, called in Venetian San Zanipolo. In the center of the field there is a beautiful wellhead from 1500, decorated with cherubs holding fruit festoons. Near the wellhead we  admired the equestrian monument of Bartolomeo Colleoni, a beautiful bronze sculpture made between 1481 and 1488 almost certainly by the Florentine artist Andrea Verocchio (1400), master of Leonardo da Vinci (1400-1500). Bartolomeo Colleoni was an important leader from Bergamo, who worked in the service of the Republic of Venice. Campo Santi Giovanni e Paolo hosts an extremely beautiful building, and it is the Scuola Grande di San Marco. As soon as you see it you will be delighted. The Scuola Grande di San Marco is one of the six Scuole Grandi in Venice, and the real masterpice of architecture. Its asymmetrical facade built of white marble, with niches and statues and a mixture of Renaissance and Byzantine style of construction confirms that it is a real gem.

Campo San Giacomo dell'Orio is a lovely and serene piazza located in Venice's Santa Croce area. This plaza, known for its quiet mood, rich foliage, and closeness to the titular church, provides a peaceful reprieve from the busy throng of more popular sites such as San Marco.

The square's name comes from the Chiesa di San Giacomo dall'Orio, a historic church on its eastern side. The church has a long history, dating back to the ninth century, with the oldest written reference coming from 1089. The word "Orio" is said to allude to the laurel trees that previously grew in the region.

Campo San Giacomo dell'Orio is a wonderful spot to rest and unwind, owing to its tranquil setting and leafy trees. It's the ideal place to escape the rush and bustle of Venice.

The Church of San Giacomo dell'Orio: This historic church is one of Venice's oldest, with amazing architecture and artwork. While the façade is very modest, the interior is filled with lovely paintings and furnishings.
Campo San Giacomo dell'Orio serves as a gathering area for residents. You may immerse yourself in Venetian culture by having a coffee or eating at one of the nearby cafés or restaurants.

A historic well in the heart of the Piazza serves as a reminder of Venice's history.

This piazza gives a peaceful respite from Venice's tourist-heavy districts, giving a more authentic and local experience.

The Chiesa di San Giacomo dall'Orio, with its rich history and distinctive architecture, is well worth a visit.

Sitting in a café and watching daily life develop is a unique way to explore Venetian culture.

If you're looking to experience Venice beyond its tourist hotspots and want to soak in the local culture, Campo San Giacomo dell'Orio is a must-visit destination. It provides a glimpse into the everyday life of Venetians and offers a refreshing change of pace from the crowded tourist areas. Whether you're interested in people-watching, enjoying a drink, or simply savoring the lively ambiance, this square is sure to leave a lasting impression.

Campo San Silvestro is a charming square located in the heart of Venice, exuding a quintessentially Venetian atmosphere. Surrounded by quaint buildings and lively cafes, it serves as a gathering place for both locals and visitors. The square is named after the nearby church of San Silvestro, adding to its historical appeal.

One of the highlights of Campo San Silvestro is the vibrant energy that fills the air, especially during the day when the sun casts a warm glow over the area. It’s an ideal spot to take a leisurely stroll, enjoy a coffee while people-watching, or simply soak in the ambiance of Venice.

While it may not be as famous as some of the larger piazzas, Campo San Silvestro offers a more intimate experience, showcasing the charm and character of the city. Overall, it's a delightful stop that encapsulates the essence of Venice’s unique blend of history and everyday life.

Piacevole campo veneziano Sestiere San Polo , ecco la storia:

In questo campo, dove abito' Aldo Manuzio il celebre tipografo del 1500, era collocata una colonna d'infamia, che indicava originariamente il luogo dove si trovava la casa di Bajamonte Tiepolo che nel 1310, assieme a Querini e Badoer, architetto' la celebre congiura in seguito alla serrata del maggior consiglio. Dopo la sconfitta di Bajamonte e dei duoi complici, la sua casa venne abbattuta ed al suo posto fu collocata nel 1364 una colonna d'infamia, che recava la seguente iscrizione: 'DE BAJAMONTE FO QUESTO TERENO E MO PER LO SO INIQUO TRADIMENTO S'e' POSTO IN CHOMUN PER ALTRUI SPAVENTO E PER MOSTRAR A TUTI SEMPRE SENO'. ( Questo terreno fu di Bajamonte Tiepolo ed ora per il suo iniquo tradimento e' diventato pubblico e queste parole siano sempre mostrate a tutti per monito agli altri ). Poco dopo il suo innalzamento la colonna venne danneggiata da un certo Francesco Fantebon che era stato un complice di Bajamonte Tiepolo e per questo condannato e poi anche graziato. A seguito del suo gesto di spregio, il Fontebon venne punito con il taglio di una mano, la perdita degli occhi e l'esilio. Anche il Tiepolo aveva il suo palazzo qui.

O Campo dei Mori é uma praça no bairro de Cannaregio , em Veneza . Seu nome vem de três estátuas de mármore, uma quarta fica ao lado da Fondamenta dei Mori, representando orientais com turbantes (adicionados posteriormente). Eles são datados do século XIII/XIV. Muitas vezes acredita-se que as três estátuas do Campo representam três irmãos gregos chamados Rioba, Sandi e Alfani, membros da família Mastelli, que se mudaram para Veneza como comerciantes no século XII. Eles foram chamados de "Mori" porque vieram da Moreia .

Después de visitar el Campo del Ghetto Nuevo, nos dirigimos a visitar la Iglesia della Madonna dell’Orto, pasamos por esta zona del Campo dei Mori muy cerca de la casa de Tintoretto. La plaza o campo toma su nombre de las estatua de los 3 moros, que corresponde a una familia de ricos comerciantes conocidos como los hermanos Mastelli, sobre estas estatuas se ha desarrollado una leyenda, la cual es curiosa, como recuerdo de la visitas no llevamos las fotos.

Statua del Turco – "Türkün Heykəli"

Venesiyada Cannaregio məhəlləsində (Madonna dell'Orto yaxınlarında) belə Osmanlı üslubunda geyinmiş bir kişi fiquru heykəli var.

Başında sarığa oxşayan papaq, əynində isə uzun şərq tərzi geyim var.

Əlində kitab və ya sənəd var – bu da onun tacir, alim və ya səfir olduğunu göstərir.

Entrando nel campo, si notano subito le quattro statue inglobate nelle pareti del palazzo. Secondo la leggenda raffigurano tre fratelli, commercianti e banchieri, dediti alla truffa, finché vennero colti dalla maledizione lanciata da una delle loro vittime e pietrificati assieme ad un loro servitore. Una delle statue, per incidente o usura, nel tempo ha perso il naso, poi sostituito da un’evidente protesi metallica.

Il campo prende nome dalle statue dei 3 mori (mori intesi come provenienti dalla Morea, attuale Peloponneso).
I 3 mori sono i fratelli Mastelli, ricchi mercanti che avevano casa (vedi palazzo Mastelli del cammello) e magazzino (vedi casa del Tintoretto) proprio in queste fondamenta (chiamate giustappunto Fondamenta dei mori).
Le statue rappresentano la pietrificazione dei 3 fratelli ad opera della Madonna, invocata da un'anziana devota, raggirata dai 3 abili mercanti truffaldini e quindi poste nel campo come eterno promemoria per i commercianti con cattive intenzioni.
La quarta statua è posizionata sulla casa del Tintoretto, ex fondaco (magazzino) dei fratelli Mastelli, e rappresenta il loro fedele servitore.

Wir haben den Campo eher zufällig entdeckt. Er liegt abseits der Hauptrouten und ist deshalb relativ ruhig. Benannt ist er nach den Statuen an den Hauswänden, den Mori. Sie sollen drei versteinerte Brüder und ihren Diener darstellen.
Die Sage erzählt, dass die Brüder zur Strafe betrogen wurden und der arme Diener gleich mit.
Tipp: "111 Orte in Venedig, die man gesehen haben muss" von Anna Toscano und Gianni Montieri (Emons 2023). Dort kann man die Sage nachlesen.

Campo San Polo, Venice's second biggest piazza after Saint Mark's Square, is a vast and attractive sanctuary located in the center of the San Polo neighborhood. This rectangular space, spanning 100 meters by 60 meters, provides a pleasant relief from the city's winding lanes.

Originally utilized as pasture and farming, the plaza was entirely paved in 1493 and ornamented with one of Venice's few wells. Throughout history, it has hosted a variety of events, including bullfights, religious speeches, and elaborate masked parties. In the seventeenth century, it was also used as a marketplace.

Today, Campo San Polo is a popular gathering spot for both locals and visitors. Venetian families frequently gather here to enjoy the open area. During the Venice Film Festival, the plaza holds open-air music and films. It also serves as a prominent location for the Venice Carnival.

If you're ready to sacrifice some sleep, an early morning visit will reward you with the entire piazza to yourself. I took full advantage of this quiet opportunity to take some breathtaking images.

This is the second largest square in Venice. Originally used as pastureland for crops and livestock, it was fully paved in 1493, with a central well added soon after. Until the 17th century, it served as a venue for local markets and a wide range of public events—including bullfights, masquerade balls, religious gatherings, and even dogfighting. Today, the square hosts major Carnival events, outdoor concerts, and film screenings during the Venice Film Festival.

Чудесная церковь 14 века, построенная в готическом стиле. Ее возвели умилиаты (орден смиренников) и посвятили святому Христофору, покровителю путешественников и корабельщиков. Она двуцветная - красный кирпич и белый известняк декоративных деталей. А в 1377 году церковь стала называться Мадонна-дель-Орто (Мадонна в саду), в честь чудотворной скульптуры сидящей Мадонны с Младенцем работы Джованни Де Санти. Статуя стояла в саду скульптора и вскоре прохожие стали замечать исходящий от нее свет, появились паломники, начались исцеления. Эта новость разлетелась по всему городу, и скульптора начали уговаривать перенести Мадонну в церковь. Так в незаконченном виде она за 150 дукатов переехала на новое место. Сейчас статую можно увидеть в правой капелле от алтаря. Церковь Мадонны в саду нераздельно связана с именем Якопо Робусти, прозванного Тинторетто, который жил неподалёку и был прихожанином этой церкви. По заказу общины он написал несколько картин, в том числе  «Поклонение золотому тельцу», «Чудо св. Агнессы», «Введение во храм», «Страшный суд». Есть в храме и работы его сына Доменико «Рождество с Ангелами». Сейчас эти шедевры украшают стены церкви. Тинторетто был человеком глубоко верующим и был похоронен в церкви в 1594 году. Вблизи алтаря, в боковом приделе справа, находится его могильная плита. Здесь же в капелле с правой стороны похоронены его дети, оба художники - сын Доменико и дочь Мариетта. В 1669 году  церковь была передана цистерцианцам Ломбардии, а в 1787 - перешла в государственное управление. В капеллах боковых нефов можно увидеть картины многих выдающихся живописцев. Некогда в капелле Валье над алтарём находилась картина Джованни Беллини  «Мадонна с Младенцем» (1481). Она была украдена из церкви в 1993 году и до сих пор не найдена. Так и осталась пустовать рама картины. Вход в церковь платный 3 евро. Время посещения - февраль 2023.

We had a wonderful stay at Hotel Ca' Del Campo with our family of four for two nights. Our room was spacious, and we were delighted to have a view of the canal right from our window. The location is fantastic, just a 5-minute walk to San Marco's, and there are plenty of excellent restaurants around.

The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful throughout our stay. We had an early flight in the morning, and they assisted us in confirming a water taxi to pick us up at 4:00 AM. They even walked with us to the deck and helped us lift our luggage onto the boat. To top it off, the staff sent us off with a bag of snacks and water since we didn't have time for breakfast.

Overall, our experience at Hotel Ca' Del Campo was exceptional, and we highly recommend it to anyone visiting the area.
